summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting primarily focused on the aftermath of a tornado that struck the community on July 6, 2022. Department heads provided updates on damage to emergency facilities, police cruisers, fire apparatus, and community infrastructure. The board took several actions including appropriating $100,000 for tornado recovery, approving demolition contracts for the damaged fire station, suspending zoning permit fees, and ratifying the emergency declaration made electronically the week prior. Community members expressed gratitude for the emergency response and mutual aid from surrounding municipalities.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to accept resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic James Mc effective July 18, 2022 - passed
Motion to promote a part-time firefighter EMT paramedic to full-time status effective July 17, 2022 - passed
Motion to rescind Resolution R-034-2022 (regarding local government fund) - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-035-2022 adopting alternate method of apportionment of undivided local government fund for calendar year 2023 - passed
Motion to increase appropriation in general fund line item 1000-110-590-0706 (Other expenses - 7622 Tornado) by $100,000 - passed
Motion to suspend zoning permit fees (related to tornado damage) - passed
Motion to approve contract with Conger Construction not to exceed $100,000 for demolition and securing of tornado-damaged Station 18 - passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-037-2022 ratifying Resolution R-036-2022 and all actions taken pursuant to the emergency declaration - passed
action_items: Austin (Brinker and Eckler counsel) and Harry (zoning inspector) to develop proposals for streamlined zoning/permit processes for tornado-affected properties and present at next Tuesday's special meeting
Cheryl to post notice for special meeting on Tuesday July 19, 2022
Administrator Pegram to enter into contract with Conger Construction for Station 18 demolition and securing
Chief Myers to apply for additional Duke Foundation grant for fire department needs
Harry and building department to work with Duke Energy and EMA to develop instructions for residents to get power restored
Staff to continue updating tornado relief page on township website and Facebook
David Barkle to follow up with township regarding motorcycle fundraiser run on August 27th route planning and coordination
Administrator Pegram to determine whether $100,000 tornado appropriation comes from general fund, fire fund, or ARP fund
key_amounts: $100,000 appropriated for tornado recovery fund (new line item 1000-110-590-0706)
$100,000 not-to-exceed contract with Conger Construction for Station 18 demolition and securing ($80,000 estimated actual cost)
Less than $10,000 additional funding from new local government fund formula for Goshen
1,971 total loads of debris removed through July 12
62 total dump trucks from 25 different municipalities assisted with cleanup
26 different mutual aid fire departments responded
Second tranche of ARP (American Rescue Plan) money received from federal government
Property tax payments received same day
notable_topics: Aftermath and recovery from July 6, 2022 tornado that damaged township emergency facilities
Fire Station 18 damage, demolition, and temporary relocation to township building basement
Police department damage and mutual aid security patrols to prevent looting
Massive multi-municipality debris removal effort (1,971 loads in one week)
FEMA aid unlikely due to small scale relative to state; state aid potentially available based on uninsured property damage
Zoning and permit process modifications for tornado-affected properties
Non-conforming structures and rebuilding regulations discussion
Local government fund redistribution formula (Resolution R-035-2022)
Community volunteer response including Lions Club, CPAA, churches, and food pantries
Unmet Needs Committee formation with county EMA, schools, and community organizations
Resignation of firefighter paramedic and promotion of part-time to full-time
Ratification of emergency electronic board meeting and state of emergency declaration
Clermont County suspended building permit fees through August; siren test scheduled for next day
Duke Energy response and grant support from Duke Foundation
attendees: Austin (Brinker and Eckler special counsel for zoning)
Tyler Bailey (Wage War Apparel business owner)
Colby (referenced as trustee, possibly from prosecutor's office)
Pastor Russ McQueen (mentioned by David Barkle)
notes: This special meeting was called in response to an EF tornado that struck Goshen Township on July 6, 2022. The tornado directly hit the township's emergency services campus, damaging the fire station (Station 18, partially collapsed), police department, administrative building, and service department facilities. Despite the severity, there were no fatalities. The township had previously held an emergency electronic meeting to declare a state of emergency since staff could not physically reach the damaged facilities. A bid opening for a planned renovation of Station 18 that was scheduled for July 7 was canceled due to the tornado damage. The meeting was highly emotional with multiple speakers fighting back tears. Clermont County permit fee suspension runs through August 8. A follow-up special meeting was scheduled for July 19, 2022 to address zoning modifications. The township's tornado shelter and command center were located in the building that was damaged, creating an unusual situation where emergency services themselves were the disaster victims.
title: Special BOT MTG 7.12.22
